items.title: Papers: West Ham's Fernandes chooses Man Utd over Arsenal
items.summary: The top stories and transfer rumours from Saturday's newspapers...

items.title: Brady stayed at West Ham despite knowing of Sullivan women's team ban
items.summary: Brady's lawyers said she had "no knowledge" of the allegations made against long-time business partner David Sullivan.

items.title: Real Madrid close in on Silva signing- Saturday's gossip
items.summary: Real Madrid expect to complete Bernardo Silva signing, Bayern Munich confident of deal for Marcus Rashford, Manchester United win race to sign Mateus Fernandes.

items.title: Ex-West Ham director would not be in involved in inquiry into David Sullivan
items.summary: Tara Warren of the Independent Football Regulator was an executive director at West Ham until December A nonexecutive director of the Independent Football Regulator will not be involved in the inquiry into allegations of sexual misconduct against David Sullivan to avoid a conflict of interest over h

items.title: Sinfield knighted as Donald, Scarratt, Murphy made OBEs
items.summary: Kevin Sinfield has been knighted in the King's Birthday Honours in recognition of his glittering rugby league career and subsequent fundraising exploits to aid research and support families affected by Motor Neurone Disease.

items.title: Sciver-Brunt hails 'ruthless' England after emphatic opening World Cup win
items.summary: Captain Nat Sciver-Brunt hailed England's ruthlessness as they kicked off the ICC Women's T20 World Cup with a 'massive' victory over Sri Lanka at Edgbaston.

items.title: 'Clear red' for Bosnia or 'wins the ball'? BBC pundits disagree
items.summary: Should Bosnia-Herzegovina have been reduced to 10 men in the second half in their 1-1 draw with Canada? Wayne Rooney and ex-referee Darren Cann had differing views.

items.title: Rooney and Cann strongly disagree on Bosnia goalkeeper incident
items.summary: Former World Cup final assistant referee Darren Cann and Wayne Rooney disagree over an incident involving Bosnia-Herzegovina goalkeeper Nikola Vasilj, with Rooney arguing the goalkeeper should have been sent off for his challenge on Canada's Tani Oluwaseyi.

items.title: Crystal Palace close to appointing Sage as manager
items.summary: Crystal Palace close to finalising a deal for Lens manager Pierre Sage to become their new head coach.

items.title: Arsenal consider move for Greek forward Tzolis
items.summary: Arsenal have been offered Club Brugge forward Christos Tzolis and are considering accelerating a move for the Greece international.

items.title: Levi Colwill breaks silence after England World Cup snub as Chelsea star jets to ‘best place in world’
items.summary: LEVI COLWILL has broken his silence after being snubbed from England's World Cup squad. The Three Lions are prepping for their tournament opener against Croatia on June 17 but the Chelsea star was one player who did not get the call. items.title: 'They might trouble England' | Part believes Spain could test Littler and Humphries
items.summary: John Part reacts to England drawing Spain in round two of the World Cup of Darts with Luke Littler and Luke Humphries gearing up to face Cristo Reyes and Jose Justicia.

items.title: Larin equaliser earns Canada first ever World Cup point
items.summary: Cyle Larin comes off the bench to score for Canada against Bosnia-Herzegovina to earn the co-hosts a 1-1 draw as they pick up their first ever point at a World Cup.

items.title: A new way to experience the World Cup - only on BBC Sport
items.summary: Ever wanted to see a World Cup match from every angle? How about a full-pitch tactical view? Now you can on the BBC.

items.title: Micah Richards makes cheeky Gary Lineker reference live on BBC for first World Cup clash
items.summary: MICAH RICHARDS took a cheeky dig at Gary Lineker immediately after returning to the BBC during the World Cup. items.title: Watch adorable Mexico-supporting DUCK as it joins crowd of fans for World Cup game
items.summary: A DUCK has shown its true colours after joining Mexico supporters for the World Cup opener. Mexico got off to a flyer as they started the tournament with a 2-0 victory over South Africa.
